header.tagline: AI that helps users fill PDF forms step by step

mobileFallback.headline: Form Copilot is best experienced on desktop

infoModal.title: AI-assisted form filling, with a human in the loop
infoModal.availableOn: Form Copilot is available on <plan>SimplePDF Pro and above</plan>.
infoModal.whyItMattersBody: The data needed to complete a form can be missing, incorrect, or invalid. This demo showcases a safer model: AI helps fill the form while a human reviews, corrects, and completes it where needed.
infoModal.humanInTheLoopBullet1: Form Copilot fills fields in real time, inside the editor.
infoModal.humanInTheLoopBullet1Hint: The data exchanged between Form Copilot and the editor happens in the browser.
infoModal.humanInTheLoopBullet2: The user can see each change, review it, and correct it.
infoModal.humanInTheLoopBullet2Hint: Form Copilot can hand off fields that should not be auto-filled, such as signatures.
infoModal.humanInTheLoopBullet3Hint: Form Copilot helps navigate the form and fill it, but the user always has the last word.
infoModal.aboutIntro: SimplePDF is built from the ground up for sensitive workflows. These three architectural choices are why.
infoModal.aboutBullet1Body: PDFs are edited locally on your device. The document content itself is not uploaded to SimplePDF servers.
infoModal.aboutBullet2Body: In production, plug in your own provider, keys, RAG pipelines, and internal data so traffic stays between your server and your AI stack.
infoModal.aboutBullet3Body: Completed documents are sent directly to your storage from the browser, ensuring that sensitive data stays in your infrastructure.
infoModal.useCasesFooter: Select a workflow above to load a different form.
infoModal.architectureSubtext: From chat to submission, the architecture is designed to keep documents and sensitive data under your control, not ours.
infoModal.architectureDemoSubtext: The document you load never leaves your browser. The data you send through the chat goes to the LLM you selected.
infoModal.architectureDemoDescription: This demo runs on a shared SimplePDF account. The browser hosts both Form Copilot and the SimplePDF editor (loaded in an iframe). Field data stays in the browser via postMessage client-side tool calls. Chat traffic flows through the demo's hosted server to a hosted AI provider, or browser-direct when you bring your own API key. The SimplePDF server records only telemetry and metadata; it never sees the document content.
infoModal.architectureDescription: The browser runs Form Copilot and the SimplePDF editor inside an iframe. The two communicate through postMessage client-side tool calls, so field data never leaves the browser. Chat messages are proxied through your server to your AI stack (provider, keys, RAG, internal data). The SimplePDF server only receives telemetry and metadata and never sees the document itself. The completed document is uploaded directly from the editor to your storage (S3, Azure Blob, or SharePoint), bypassing the SimplePDF server. When a submission completes, an optional webhook can notify your server. The webhook carries metadata only, never the document content.

tax.title: Tax
tax.body: Tax forms like the IRS W-9 combine identity fields, classification choices, and a signature. Form Copilot helps fill the identity section, explains each classification option, and leaves the signature to the user.

hr.title: HR onboarding
hr.body: Employment eligibility forms like the USCIS I-9 mix free-text identity fields with attestations and tight formatting rules. Form Copilot fills what it can, flags the sections that need a human review, and hands off for signatures.

healthcare.title: Healthcare
healthcare.body: Patient intake and claims forms often contain sensitive data and require review. Form Copilot helps complete the form step by step using your organization's approved AI provider.

insurance.title: Insurance
insurance.body: Applications, claims, and endorsements often combine structured data with policy-specific details. Form Copilot helps prefill what it can, explain fields, and leaves the final review to the user.

chat.heading: Form Copilot
chat.subtitleWaiting: Waiting for the editor to load…
chat.subtitleNoModel: Bring your own AI to start chatting

modelPicker.title: Bring your own AI
modelPicker.byokIntro: Pick a provider and paste your key to chat through it instead.
modelPicker.byokSecurityTitle: Your credentials stay on your device.
modelPicker.byokSecurityBody: Credentials are held in memory only (never written to disk, never sent to SimplePDF's servers) and every LLM request is fired straight from your browser to the AI you chose.
modelPicker.keyInputHint: The key never leaves your browser. Stored in memory for this tab only.
modelPicker.keyInvalid: This API key was rejected by the provider.
modelPicker.keyModelNotFound: Model not found. Pick a different model or check that your plan includes it.
modelPicker.keyNetworkError: Could not reach the provider to verify the key. Check your connection and try again.
modelPicker.registerInterestMessage:
Hi SimplePDF team, I'm interested in using Form Copilot with {{provider}}. Could you share when it'll be available and what the setup looks like?

Plan I'm interested in:
- [ ] PRO
- [ ] PREMIUM
modelPicker.customBaseUrlHint: Any OpenAI-compatible endpoint (Ollama, LM Studio, vLLM, self-hosted). Your server must allow CORS from this page.
modelPicker.customKeyPlaceholder: Your API key (or any string for local setups)
modelPicker.customKeyHint: Runs browser-direct against the URL above. Use a localhost URL to keep everything on your machine.
modelPicker.customReachFailed: Could not reach the endpoint. Check the URL, and that your server allows CORS from this page.
modelPicker.customModelNotFound: Model not found on this server. Check the model name (and that it has been pulled / loaded).
modelPicker.customAuthRejected: The server rejected the API key. Check the key, or leave it blank for local setups that do not enforce auth.

chat.piiWarning: Public demo. Use sample data only. Messages are processed by the selected AI provider.
chat.newFieldHint: New {{field}} field added by the user
chat.errorAuthBody: The AI provider rejected your API key. <switchModel>Open the AI model selection</switchModel> to verify the key, and try again.
chat.errorRateLimitedBodyThanks: Thanks for trying it. This demo uses hosted AI, so usage is capped.
chat.errorRateLimitedBodyCta: Use your own API key to keep filling forms.
chat.shareHero: AI filling a PDF in real time is better seen than explained.
chat.shareCtaLabel: Send it to someone who should try it:
chat.shareTweetText: I've been trying out Form Copilot by SimplePDF, an AI that fills PDF forms step by step. Worth a look:
chat.welcomeTitle: Try Form Copilot with your own AI
chat.welcomeBody: Your credentials stay in this tab. Requests are sent from your browser to your selected AI provider.

download.title: Download your filled PDF
download.intro: Your PDF is generated in this browser tab. Nothing leaves your device, and SimplePDF's servers never see the filled file.
download.upsellStorage: On paid SimplePDF plans, this Download becomes a Submit flow. Filled PDFs land automatically in your own storage (S3, Azure Blob Storage, or SharePoint), and your systems get notified via webhook or email. Download stays optional.
download.upsellFields: PDF forms are messy. They arrive scanned without ready-made fields, or with bad field names that make the AI work harder. Paid SimplePDF plans fix that too, with required fields, default values, and field aliasing.
download.upsellContactMessage:
Hi SimplePDF team, I'm trying Form Copilot and I'd like to learn more about the Submit flow on paid plans (automatic delivery to my own storage, plus webhook / email notifications).

Plan I'm interested in:
- [ ] PRO
- [ ] PREMIUM

forms.customSubtitle: Any PDF works - kept on your device
